Hollowmere

The Drowned Vigil — a D&D 5e simulation MMO, live in the browser

server up · e2e 14/14 · autopilot: VICTORY + DIALOGUE VISIBLE
Play it now: on your PC, open http://localhost:8060/index.html in Chrome. Click Connect, forge a character, click the ground to walk. Click once on the music toggle or anywhere after loading and the score comes up (the browser holds audio until your first click). J = journal, C = character sheet, I = inventory, Enter = chat, 1–5 = action bar.

What today's eyes-on pass caught

The automated QA has been green for days — it plays the whole loop over the network and checks every rule. But it reads logs, not pixels. Actually looking at frames caught five bugs the logs never could:

  • The ground was invisible. The terrain atlas was 27,904 px wide — browsers cap textures at 16,384. Repacked into a grid; the world has land again.
  • The whole HUD hugged the corner. Godot quietly refuses to size runtime UI roots under a CanvasLayer — every screen was laying out against a 0×0 rectangle. Now pinned to the viewport.
  • The action bar crashed silently on a missing dictionary key that release builds swallow.
  • Ghost characters loitered at spawn — offline test bots now stay invisible.
  • The rat pack camped the village well and killed every newcomer (twice, on camera). Relocated to the far hedge, per level design, not rule-bending.

The autopilot's last run

[HM][QA] target rat #30 — closing in [HM][QA] in range — casting fire_bolt [HM][JUICE] attack anim fired · hit flash fired [HM][QA] ✅ VICTORY: rat slain. gold=2 — FULL LOOP COMPLETE [HM][QA] hurt (hp=1) — drinking a potion before the walk [HM][QA] at Maera — interacting [HM][HUD] dialogue node=intro options=4 [HM][QA] ✅ DIALOGUE VISIBLE — QA COMPLETE
